phase_name: Integrate OddsBlaze API as new data source

completed_tasks.name: Backend — API client, DB migration, config, and all wiring
Frontend — Add OddsBlaze status column to dashboard

blockers.description: OddsBlaze push feed (SSE) returns 'Invalid key' — requires Enterprise plan upgrade
blockers.workaround: Using REST polling at 30s interval instead

blockers.description: DB had pre-existing odds_blaze_status column (underscore) from prior attempt — renamed to oddsblaze_status via ALTER TABLE

decisions.rationale: OddsBlaze has no published rate limits and data refreshes ~every 2 minutes on their end; 30s gives near-real-time without risk

decisions.decision: REST polling over SSE push feed
decisions.rationale: Current API key doesn't have Enterprise plan access for push feed

decisions.decision: Status derivation: live=true -> 'live', live=false + past 3hrs -> 'final', else -> 'scheduled'
decisions.rationale: OddsBlaze only provides a boolean 'live' field, no explicit completed status

next_action: Monitor OddsBlaze matching quality — check if fuzzy matching is correctly pairing OddsBlaze events to ProphetX events across all sports. Consider tuning the match threshold if too many events show 'Not Listed' for covered sports.
context_notes: OddsBlaze integration fully deployed to production server (46.225.233.32). All 8 Docker services running. 106 events matched on first poll (NBA, MLB, UFC, MLS, EPL, Ligue 1, La Liga). 599 events unmatched — mostly tennis/hockey which OddsBlaze doesn't cover. Old duplicate migration file (007_add_odds_blaze_status.py) was cleaned up on server. Column was renamed from odds_blaze_status to oddsblaze_status directly in production DB.
